ques: As a part of the preparation for bunkering, we are to empty the fuel oil overflow tank before the bunkering. Why?
A: A: Check that there are no leaks from valve manifold, into overflow tank
B: B: In case of an overfilling.
C: C: To avoid mixing new and old bunker oil
D: D: Check that there are no leaks from bunker tanks, into overflow tank.

ques: Question: A 2 stroke engine is usually equipped with an auxiliary blower fan, which is supposed to be used:
A: A. continuously, together with the turbochargers to ensure enough air to the engine during full speed running.
B: B. during a scavenging fire, to help put out the fire.
C: C. during manoeuvre and manoeuvre speed condition (arrival and departure)
D: D. during continuously reduced speed under a longer sea voyage.

ques: Question: A Cargo Ship Construction Certificate will be issued for:
A: A period not exceeding 6 months
B: A period not exceeding 24 months
C: A period not exceeding 12 months
D: A period not exceeding 48 months

ques: Question: A pan on the galley stove containing oil catches fire, which of the following should NOT be used on this type of fire?
A: A. Raise the alarm, close galley door and wait for the fire teams to arrive.
B: B. Use water extinguisher to put out the fire.
C: C. Cover pan with lid and switch of heat source.
D: D. Cover pan with fire blanket and switch of heat source.

ques: Question: A transformer with 10:1 turns ratio and rated 50 kVA, 2400/240 Volts, 60 Hz, is used to step down the voltage to a distribution system. The low tension voltage is to be kept constant at 240 Volts. What load impedance connected to the low-tension side will cause the transformer to be fully loaded?
A: 1.15 Ohms
B: 115 Ohms
C: C) 2.3 Ohms
D: D) 230 Ohms

ques: Question: After complete shut down of the steam plant, the boiler have been heated in accordance to correct starting up procedure, and is now firing at working pressure. You are going to open the main steam valve to the system. What will be the correct procedure for this operation?
A: A. Open as fast as possible to get rid of water assembly in the piping system.
B: B. Don't open the main valve before you have top pressure on the boiler.
C: C. Open steam valve very slowly, considering system expansion.
D: D. Open as fast as possible to heat the piping system.

ques: Question: Among working conditions listed below which one is required for every propulsion engine of lifeboats?

ques: Question: An important quantity which is useful in circuit analysis is known as conductance G (Siemens). Which of the formulas A to D expresses the conductance for this circuit?
A: G=1/R

C: G=R*I

ques: Question: As a fire fighter, what should you always consider when fighting a fire in a confined space?
A: A. The confined space has only five sides
B: B. The confined space can be considered to have one side only as heat always rises
C: C. The confined space has only four sides
D: D. The confined space always has six sides

ques: Question: At which point do you engage the main circuit breaker of the incoming alternator when paralleling two alternators?

ques: Question: Before engaging the Turning Gear, precautions should be taken to:
A: A. Close the inlet and outlet valves for cooling water
B: B. Transfer M/E control from control room to emergency control console of engine
C: C. Open the indicator cocks and open the starting air supply
D: D. Shut off the starting air supply and open the indicator cocks

ques: Question: Before entering tanks and / or enclosed spaces - what should be done?
A: A. Test the atmosphere of the space by approved method.
B: B. Notify the master or the responsible officer.
C: C. All of the alternatives together.
D: D. Agree with those entering the space on a signal system.

ques: Question: Due to exposure to heat fatigue, heat stroke and dehydration, what is the maximum recommended effective temperature (ET) for full work load in enclosed spaces?
Question: Due to failure of one of the carbon brushes the excitation voltage is lost on one alternator that is operating in parallel. Will that alternator then:
A: A) Continue to share the active load (kW) but have very high reactive load (kVAR)
B: B) Overload due to reduced voltage output and increased current
C: C) Try to take all the load and so trip the main circuit breaker
D: D) Completely loose its share of the load causing the auxiliary engine to speed up

ques: Question: During a routine check of one of the auxiliary engines in an UMS operated engine room, a minor leakage on a high pressure fuel pipe was observed. What would be an appropriate action and/or precaution to be taken? (Assume normal operation; 2 sets operating and 1 set standby)
A: A. Have a motorman on watch for the rest of the night, especially instructed to watch the engine
B: B. Any of the mentioned alternatives will meet the requirement.
C: C. Change the operation to the third generator set, leaving the original set as stand-by awaiting possibility to repair the failure.
D: D. Change operation to the third generator set, preparing the original set for repair of the failure and switching to manual mode until repair is carried out.

ques: Question: During synchronising, the incoming generator should be running slightly "fast" compared to the busbar frequency. This is to ensure that the:
A: A Incomers reverse power trip is tested
B: B Incomer picks up as a generator
C: C Incomer picks up as a motor
D: D Most rapid synchronising action is achieved

ques: Question: How does low expansion foam act as an extinguishing agent when dealing with oil fires?
A: A. By cooling only
B: B. By smothering only

D: D. By smothering and also by providing some cooling

ques: Question: How many bolts do you have to have in the blinds on the bunker lines when not in use?

ques: Question: How often shall the oxygen analyser be calibrated?

ques: Question: One of the Main Engine cooling pumps is down for maintenance. The rest of the cooling pumps are operating on full capacity with minimum margins on the maximum temperature limit. What will be the correct precaution or action to take at the end of the day?

ques: Question: You are Sen.Off.Deck on a laden tanker which has collided with a smaller vessel. What should your first action be?
